en.title: Text Classification
en.prompt: Classify the following keyword list into groups based on their search intent, whether commercial, transactional or informational: [keywords]
en.remark: According to the search intent, group the following keyword list into commercial, transactional or informational search intent.

related.title: language literary critic
related.remark: Analyzing and interpreting literary works, while providing their origins and influence.

related.title: lunatic
related.remark: Play the role of a crazy person and reply with meaningless and illogical sentences.

related.title: plagiarism checker
related.remark: Determine whether the input sentence exists in the ChatGPT database.

metaDescription: Group SEO keywords by Commercial, Transactional, or Informational intent. Target the right audience with precise sorting.

faq.q: What is the difference between commercial, transactional, and informational intent?
faq.a: Commercial = the user is comparing options (like "XX review"); transactional = the user is ready to buy (like "XX official site download"); informational = the user is seeking knowledge (like "what is XX"). When planning SEO content, each intent maps to a different page type and mixing them loses focus.

faq.q: What if one keyword could belong to two categories at once?
faq.a: Append "if a keyword's intent is ambiguous, label the primary and secondary intent, and provide one suitable content suggestion for each." The AI defaults to stuffing everything into one category, and long-tail keywords with dual "informational + transactional" intent end up missing their commercial value.
